message.content:
You are a code reviewer. Review the CashbackInvoiceItem type definition and its usage.

Context: The InvoiceCashbackOnboarding screen was updated to use CashbackInvoiceItem instead of InformationList. 

Check:
1. Read the CashbackInvoiceItem type in /Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/models/index.ts
2. Verify the type definition uses proper design system types (PropsIconsNameCore, PropsTokenColorsCore, TextIconType)
3. Check if the invoiceList() function in index.tsx properly returns items matching CashbackInvoiceItem
4. Verify the 'type' field uses valid TextIconType values (should be 'default')
5. Verify the 'icon' field uses valid PropsIconsNameCore values
6. Verify the iconColor and iconColorShape fields use valid PropsTokenColorsCore values

Provide feedback on any type mismatches or potential issues.

content.text: Now let me verify the design system types to ensure the values used are valid.

content.text: Now let me check the view file to see how the CashbackInvoiceItem is being used.

content.content:
1→import React, { FC } from 'react';
     2→import { InvoiceCashbackViewProps } from './models';
     3→import { Box, Text, ListTextIcon } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
     4→import BasicScreen from '../../atomic/templates/BasicScreen';
     5→import { Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
     6→import { Skeleton } from '../../components/TransactionsListFactory/styles';
     7→
     8→const InvoiceCashbackView: FC<InvoiceCashbackViewProps> = ({
     9→  items,
    10→  hasError,
    11→  hasLoading,
    12→  handleGoBack,
    13→  handleContinue,
    14→  handleRetry,
    15→  cashbackInvoiceList,
    16→  isLoadingParameterization,
    17→  width,
    18→}) => {
    19→  return (
    20→    <BasicScreen
    21→      hasScroll
    22→      hasHeader
    23→      bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
    24→      leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
    25→      title="Cashback na fatura"
    26→      hasRightIcon={false}
    27→      errorButtonOnPress={handleRetry}
    28→      isLoading={hasLoading}
    29→      hasError={hasError}
    30→    >
    31→      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
    32→        <Text
    33→          color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
    34→          testID="invoice-cashback-main-title"
    35→          type="headingMDL"
    36→          mt="smaller"
    37→        >
    38→          Use pontos para pagar menos na fatura
    39→        </Text>
    40→      </Box>
    41→      {isLoadingParameterization &&
    42→        cashbackInvoiceList?.map?.((item) => (
    43→          <Box pd="innerLarge" key={item.text}>
    44→            <Skeleton width={width - 40} height={60} />
    45→          </Box>
    46→        ))}
    47→      {!isLoadingParameterization && (
    48→        <>
    49→          <Box ml="nano" mr="nano" flex={1} justify="flex-start">
    50→            <ListTextIcon items={items} ml="nano" mr="xs" mt="xxs" type="list" />
    51→          </Box>
    52→
    53→          <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
    54→            <Button
    55→              text="Continuar"
    56→              mt="large"
    57→              mb="small"
    58→              onPress={handleContinue}
    59→              testID="invoice-cashback-button"
    60→            />
    61→          </Box>
    62→        </>
    63→      )}
    64→    </BasicScreen>
    65→  );
    66→};
    67→
    68→export default InvoiceCashbackView;
    69→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.text: Let me check the ListTextIcon component to understand what props it expects for its items.
